Dashboard Archive: 2026-03-12 Home
About This Dashboard
What It Does
Long-term momentum ranker scoring 700+ tickers on returns from 1 month to 10 years, with relative performance vs SPY over 1y/5y/10y. Same scoring engine as the short-term ranker but optimized for longer investment horizons and secular trends.
Today's Reading
# Market Analysis: Momentum-Ranker-Long Today's data shows two exceptionally strong performers at the top of the long-term momentum rankings. NOC (Northrop Grumman) ranks #1 with a score of 92.5, driven by consistent multi-year gains including 49.71% over 1 year and 285% over 10 years, with all four moving average signals aligned and outperformance of 29.67% versus SPY over the past year. CLMT shows even more aggressive returns with 169% over 1 year and 566% over 5 years, though its 10-year return of 167% and weak 10y/5y ratio of 0.29 suggest it's a more recent high-flyer that underperformed SPY by 67% over the decade. Both stocks pass their moving average tests and show "clean" momentum signals, but CLMT's extreme relative strength over shorter periods combined with deteriorating long-term ratios indicates a potential valuation or sector rotation risk that contrasts with NOC's more balanced secular trend performance. The correlation profiles differ notably: NOC ties closely to industrial and energy sectors (XLI, XLE) while maintaining distance from tech, whereas CLMT data appears truncated but shows broader market-beating patterns across recent time horizons.
